An iconic 605-ft-tall spire at the Seattle 
Center Participants, will have the 
opportunity to walk the observation deck.
The Pike Place Market is one of the oldest and largest 
operating public markets in the United States. The 
city is committed to preserving, rehabilitating, and 
protecting the Market’s buildings; increasing farm 
and food retailing opportunities; incubating and 
supporting small and marginal businesses; and 
providing services for low-income people. 
ACE Delegates will be provided an overview of the 
Market and have time to explore.
While visiting the culture, art, and history museum 
located in Seattle’s Chinatown-International District, 
ACE delegates will have the opportunity to learn about 
how Seattle’s Asian and Asian American community, 
the region’s largest racial minority, has contributed to 
the region’s economic development.

The Columbia Tower is the tallest building in Seattle 
with 360-degree views of the region. The Columbia 
Center Seattle is made up of 76 stories, which are 
primarily leased as office space, and presents rental 
and investment opportunities. 
An Overview of the Greater Seattle Region 
Economic Development Ecosystem
A conversation about workforce development, AI and 
other emerging technologies, women in tech, and 
sustainability practices. 
Participants will learn about the Spring District, which 
was developed in the 1960s for light industrial uses and 
warehousing. It is now redeveloped into a mixed-use 
neighborhood. As part of the visit to the Spring District, 
participants will learn and connect with the University of 
Washington’s Global Innovation Exchange (GIX) which is 
a university-industry nexus for interdisciplinary education 
in technology innovation. In partnership with corporate, 
government, and non-profit organizations, GIX delivers 
transformational learning experiences through world-class 
graduate and professional development programs. 
ACE Delegates will be able to identify partnership 
opportunities 
for 
prototyping 
labs 
and 
student 
exchanges at the GIX, an incubator that inspires 
innovators to solve real-world challenges through the 
responsible 
application 
of 
advanced 
technologies. 
https://wrightrunstad.com/
Delegates will learn about the Washington Wine Industry, 
followed by opportunities to network with local officials and 
explore Chateau Ste. Michelle.
Load Buses and Transfer from
 Columbia Tower to the 
 University of Washington
Load Buses and Transfer from 
the University of Washington 
to Microsoft 
The University of Washington is one of the world’s 
preeminent public universities. Ranked No. 7 in the 
world on the U.S. News & World Report’s Best Global 
Universities rankings.
 Featured Initiative: CoMotion 
https://comotion.uw.edu/about-us/
 
Delegates will learn and connect with the University’s 
collaborative innovation hub which focuses on innovation 
training; IP advising and protection; funding and mentorship; 
and licensing, startup and incubation. They will also learn about 
the Mobility Innovation Center, which supports the progress of 
Seattle as a global leader in smart city transportation.
 Featured Initiative: Clean Energy Institute 
https://www.wcet.washington.edu/
 
Delegates will explore partnership opportunities with the 
University of Washington Clean Energy Institute, the inventors 
of the Washington Clean Energy Testbeds, which accelerate the 
development, scaling-up, and adoption of new technologies in 
solar harvesting, energy storage, and system integration. This 
open-access facility in Seattle was founded on the principle 
that users retain intellectual property. It offers customized 
training and use of instruments for fabricating prototypes, 
testing devices and modules, and integrating systems.

Participants will visit the tech and innovation field 
labs, a dynamic development platform that facilitates 
collaborative experimentation for commercially 
viable entrepreneurship. It is a true collaborative 
public-private environment for startups, with top 
level solutions delivering all the capabilities of a real 
enterprise platform. Snohomish County Food and 
Farming Center will be featured. 
There will be companies showcased and time for 
engagement between the participants and 5G 
Agricultural Field Labs. 
Hosted by: 
Snohomish County Executive Dave Somers 
City of Arlington Mayor Barbara Tolbert.
A unique opportunity to network with hosts and the 
visiting delegation.
Transfer to
 Port of Everett
Transfer to
 Paine Field
Delegates will learn how the Port has created 
economic opportunities and jobs while providing 
maritime and marine-related services and appropriate 
public access to the waterfront. Trade and investment 
opportunities in supply chain logistics, real estate, 
and tourism will be presented. There will also be a 
discussion about how the Port interacts with the rest 
of Seattle’s economic development ecosystem.
Through its international seaport, public marina, 
and real estate development operations, the Port 
of Everett brings jobs, business, and tourism to the 
community and region. Port activities support more 
than 40,000 jobs in the surrounding community and 
contributes $433 million in state and local taxes. 
Tour the Boeing manufacturing facility and 
neighboring ATS/Commercial terminal. 
As 
a 
leading 
global 
aerospace 
company, 
Boeing develops, manufactures, and services 
commercial airplanes, defense products, and 
space systems for customers in more than 150 
countries. As a top U.S. exporter, the company 
leverages the talents of a global supplier base 
to advance economic opportunity, sustainability, 
and community impact.

The Research Center for Urban 
Waters Presents Opportunities 
to Access Research Labs, Receive 
Technical Assistance, and 
Explore Research Exchanges in 
Environmental Monitoring, Testing, 
Restoration, and Stewardship
Innovation and technology will be featured during 
the visit to the Joint Base Lewis-McChord, which 
provides state-of-the-art training and infrastructure, 
responsive quality of life programs, and fully-
capable mobilization and deployment operations 
for the Army, Navy, Air Force, and Marines.
Transfer to
 Port of Tacoma
Investments 
promote 
prosperity, 
trade, 
and 
jobs, while also protecting and bolstering the 
environment. Delegates will be able to identify 
potential opportunities in supply chain logistics 
and trade. 
During the visit, ACE delegates will learn how the 
University of Washington - Tacoma Campus - impacts 
regional economic development and interacts with 
the overall ecosystem (academia-industry, academia-
government collaboration). Featured programs will 
include the machine-learning competition, women 
in STEM and education program, and the business 
diversity and equity program.
University of Washington Tacoma is one of the 
three campuses that make up the University of 
Washington. UW Tacoma serves an urban community 
and provides access to education for students in 
a way that transforms families and communities. 
It impacts and informs economic development 
through community-engaged students and faculty. It 
also conducts research that is of direct use to Seattle’s 
community and region
Hosted by: 
City of Tacoma Deputy Mayor Kristina Walker.
Pierce County Executive Bruce Dammeier 
 
The Museum of Glass is a premier contemporary art 
museum dedicated to glass and glassmaking in the 
West Coast’s largest and most active museum glass 
studio. The Museum has established a reputation for 
hosting impactful and engaging artist residencies, 
organizing and exhibiting traveling exhibitions, 
and creating unique programs for visitors, all while 
building a growing permanent collection chronicling 
the development of modern and contemporary 
art glass.
The Research Center for Urban Waters propels 
collaboration 
between 
scientists, 
analysts, 
engineers, and policy makers to restore and protect 
waterways.The unique intellectual environment 
brings together organizations with complementary 
missions and individuals with diverse skills to 
develop innovative approaches to environmental 
restoration and protection as well as sustainable 
urban development.

Highlighting sustainability projects, the tourism 
sector, and technology collaboration opportunities 
over breakfast. Delegates will have the opportunity 
to hear and connect with representatives from 
the Washington State University (WSU), the Port of 
Seattle, and Alaska Airlines.
Constructed in 1962 to house employees involved in 
the construction of the Space Needle, the Edgewater 
evolved into the only over-water hotel in Seattle. It has 
been host to litany of famous musicians in its history. 
Travel and Leisure rated the hotel top 5 in Seattle.
Transfer to
 Ferry
Transfer to SAFE BOATS & the
 Port of Bremerton
Travel to Bremerton in Kitsap County
The Suquamish is one of many Indian Tribes in 
the Pacific Northwest. Taking their name from 
the 
traditional 
Lushootseed 
language 
phrase 
for “people of the clear salt water” these expert 
fisherman, canoe builders and basket weavers have 
lived in harmony with the lands and waterways 
along Washington’s Central Puget Sound Region 
for thousands of years.The abundance of natural 
resources and efficient technology for harvesting 
and preserving food enabled them to develop a rich 
cultural and spiritual life.
Built by the Suquamish Tribe in 2008, the House 
of Awakened Cultures acts as a spiritual successor 
to the Old Man House, which was destroyed 1870 
to disperse the tribe. Since its opening in 2009, the 
house has served as a community center for the 
Suquamish Tribe and the community. Construction 
of the House of Awakened Culture was a component 
of a larger revitalization campaign for the Suquamish 
Tribe, which also included construction of a new 
Suquamish Museum.
Hosted by: 
Chairman Leonard Forsman, Suquamish Tribe
Have an optional evening break and/or dinner on 
your own with like-minded delegates for informal 
networking. Meet in the Renaissance Seattle Hotel 
main lobby and then head over to a local restaurant 
of your choice. Book your reservations and get a taste 
of Seattle’s city life.
Travel via Bus to Bainbridge Ferry to Seattle
Kitsap Conference Center at Bremerton Harborside 
offers 17,000 square feet of meeting and event space 
on the waterfront of Bremerton, Washington. Just a 
short ferry ride from Seattle, Kitsap Conference Center 
offers an unsurpassed standard of excellence for your 
meetings, conferences, trade shows, social events, 
reunions, and weddings in Washington State. 
SAFE 
Boats 
International 
received 
well-earned 
recognition as Manufacturer of the Year from the 
Association of Washington Business (AWB). The 
company employs highly qualified experts to build 
the boats and equip these professionals with the latest 
manufacturing tools and technology. 
SAFE Boats is present in over 50 countries around the 
world, with expansion into new regions every year. It 
has served internationally: Chilean Navy, Mexican Navy, 
Nigerian Navy, Pakistan Navy, Tunisian Coast Guard, 
Bangladesh Navy, Colombian Navy, Colombian Coast 
Guard, and more.
Boats are designed to assist federal agencies, state and 
local law enforcement, military, and fire and rescue.

The Amazon Spheres (also called the Seattle 
Spheres) is a collaborative indoor garden which is 
an icon of downtown Seattle, but also a space for 
Amazon employees to connect with nature and 
spur innovation at its headquarters. Tropical plants 
from South America, Asia, Africa, and Australia fill 
various themed spaces. 
Delegates will be able to learn and connect 
with key stakeholders from the space industry in 
Seattle and explore cooperation opportunities. 
The Museum of Flight is the largest independent, 
non-profit air and space museum in the 
world. With over 175 aircraft and spacecraft, 
tens of thousands of artifacts, millions of rare 
photographs, dozens of exhibits and experiences 
and a world-class library, the museum and its 
people bring mankind’s incredible history of flight 
to life. The Museum of Flight exists to acquire, 
preserve and exhibit historically significant air and 
space artifacts, which provide a foundation for 
scholarly research and lifelong learning programs 
that inspire an interest in and understanding of 
science, technology, and the humanities.
Delegates will taste ethically-sourced coffee from 
over 400,000 farmers in countries worldwide and 
learn how Starbucks supports the farmers and 
local communities where their coffee is grown.
Chihuly Garden and Glass opened in 2012. Through 
the work of artist Dale Chihuly, the museum 
celebrates the Puget Sound region’s creative energy 
and inspires visitors to engage with our region’s 
cultural community.
ACE leaders will have the opportunity to tour the
facility, learn about the research center, and Explore 
cooperation opportunities with representatives.
Fred Hutchinson Cancer Center unites innovative 
research and compassionate care to prevent
and eliminate cancer and infectious disease.They are 
driven by the urgency of their patients, the hope of their 
community and their passion for discovery to pursue 
scientific breakthroughs and healthier lives for every 
person in every community. The research center opened 
in 1975 after Bill Hutchinson’s brother Fred Hutchinson 
was diagnosed and died from cancer.
Welcome and panel session at the Behnke Conference 
Suite. Break into smaller groups to tour the facility.
